observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management:

Advise the Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers. 

Initiate the request for a write-off on other receivables that remained non-moving for almost ten (10) years or more in the books, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: No request for write-off of the dormant accounts has been made due to unavailability of documents/ records evidencing the financial transactions.

observations.recommendation:
1. Management enjoin the requesting offices to submit their DVs, OBRs, and supporting documents on time to facilitate the Municipal Accountant’s preparation and submission of the required reports to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
2. The Municipal Budget Officer take steps to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the SAAOB. This could entail hiring temporary staff during busy periods or re-evaluating the workflow and deadlines to accommodate unforeseen delays.

3. Management observe the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to have reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability as follows:
a. Reorganize the Inventory Committee to include members who can be relieved of their regular duties to devote full time to conducting the physical inventory taking until the same is completed.
b. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at the station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items and require the Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile their records based on the results of the actual physical inventory and effect necessary adjustments;
c. Prepare and submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period; and
d. Undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs(PPE Ledger Cards) maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled.

4. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to focus on the physical count of local roads; and
5. Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee had not yet been reorganized.

Management commented that there is difficulty in implementing the perpetual inventory method due to a lack of storage space for inventories purchased and insufficient personnel to handle and distribute inventories.

observations.recommendation: 11. Management provide additional manpower in the Municipal Engineering Office and the necessary equipment to hasten the implementation of the PPAs under the 20% DF.
observations.reason: Management provided a resolution to the Audit Team regarding existing balances from completed projects.

observations.recommendation:
12. Management reallocate the funds from completed and unimplemented projects that the Municipality can no longer execute to finance disaster risk reduction and management activities pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-02, dated September 12, 2012.
13. The Municipal Accountant reclassify and transfer the identified MOOE account amounting to ₱200,000.00 to the Trust Liabilities - Special Trust Fund account.
observations.reason: The audit team did not receive proof of reclassification and transfer, as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
16. The LDRRM pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2011 and 2013 LDRRMF unspent balances to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan through the Local Development Council.
17. The members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the reversion and certify the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for social services. Henceforth, they should annually review the Special Trust Fund to determine the available amounts for reversion.
observations.reason: The audit team was not given the supporting documents for the recommendations that had already been implemented.

observations.recommendation:
18. Management provide the needed manpower requirement for the inventory committee to complete the physical count of PPE in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, and direct the Accounting and Property Units to reconcile the results of the physical count with the related property and accounting records pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to ensure the accuracy of the reported PPE balances in the financial statements.
19. The Municipality avail the one-time cleansing of non-existing or missing PPE items in accordance with Section 8.0 of the same Circular to ensure that reported PPE balances are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

20. The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant trace the differences in the Cash Local Treasury account.
21. Henceforth, conduct properly the quarterly reconciliation between the treasury records as against accounting records in compliance with Section 143 (A.7) of the LTOM.
observations.reason: Management commented that reconciliation was still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
22. The Municipal Accountant submit the subsidiary ledgers of each accountable officer as of December 31, 2021, monitor cash advances, and demand the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances by issuing demand letters direct to the accountable officers concerned. Otherwise, if no liquidation is made, impose the sanction of withholding the salaries of those who still fail to settle their accounts after due notice.
23. The Municipal Mayor require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their cash advances and direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period and see to it that no additional cash advances are granted unless previous ones are settled or properly accounted for.
observations.reason: The designated disbursing officer had not yet settled the unliquidated cash advances and the disbursing officer had not yet settled the unliquidated advances.

observations.recommendation:
24. Municipal Accountant to strictly observe compliance with the perpetual inventory method of accounting for construction supplies and materials procured for projects implemented by the administration by recording all purchases under the “Construction Materials Inventory.”
25. The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the property custodian to implement controls on the receipt and utilization of the same, submit a summary of the utilized construction materials to the Accounting Office, and effect the necessary adjustment for the cost of supplies and materials which remained unutilized for the intended projects.
26. Management take up a receivable account from the water associations for the amount incurred by the Municipality for the improvement of the water systems.
observations.reason: The necessary adjusting entry had not yet been taken up in the books.
The PSCP has not yet been prepared or submitted.

observations.recommendation:
29. Management require the suppliers/dealers to submit the Tax and Duty Receipt corresponding to the equipment procured in accordance with Section 2 of A.O. No. 200 dated November 21, 1990.
a. Tax receipts from the Bureau of Customs or the BIR were not attached to the DVs nor submitted to the Audit Team as required under Section 2 of A.O. No. 200 dated November 21, 1990.
b. The Municipality failed to deduct one percent (1%) warranty security from the total contract price of the vehicles, posing the risk that if there are defects in the vehicles, the LGU may not be able to recover the cost thereof.

observations.recommendation:

31. Likewise, adequate disclosure shall be provided in the Notes to FS for the details of the loans payable pursuant to paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

Management prepare and furnish the Auditor with a copy of the (a) acknowledgment receipts of the donations in-kind; (b) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (c) inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

38. Management submit to the Auditor a copy of the (a) Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), (b) Report on the Sources of Fund and Utilization, (c) photocopy of the identification card with specimen signature of the beneficiaries, (d) photos of the beneficiaries during actual receipt of the assistance, and (e) a copy of the Official Receipt for any refund of the unutilized balance.

The BAC require the suppliers to submit the original notarized OSS before or after the award of the contract but before payment is made and include a warranty clause in the contract or POs, for procurements through emergency purchase in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
43. Management comply with the required five days remaining leave credits after monetization in accordance with Section 22 of Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of E.O. No. 292 dated July 2010.
44. Management require the personnel listed in Annex 10 who availed of monetization of 50% or more of their accumulated leave credits to submit proof that the proceeds thereof were used for any of the reasons cited in the Omnibus Rule.
45. The Municipal Accountant ensure that monetization of leave credits are properly supported with valid documents in compliance with Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, before processing the payment.
46. VL credits should be exhausted first before SL credits are used in accordance with CSC Resolution No. 00-0034 dated January 5, 2000, and that monetization of leave credits shall be availed of only once a year pursuant to CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 25-97 dated June 27, 1997.

observations.recommendation:

48. Management strictly comply with the submission of the AAPSI within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation:
49. The Municipal Agriculturist conduct an inventory of breeding stocks and render a report thereon, including the fair market value of each stock, to the Municipal Accountant, for proper recording in the books of accounts.
50. The Municipal Agriculturist keep a systematic record of births, deaths, and disposals, of breeding stocks. In case of death or disposal, he shall facilitate the request for relief from accountability in accordance with Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92- 386 dated October 20, 1992, for dropping the account from the books.
51. Proper disclosure shall be provided in the Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with PPSAS No. 27 to provide relevant information that might affect the fair presentation of the financial statements.

52. The Municipal Accountant (a) conduct a thorough verification and analysis of the dormant receivable accounts and provide for an allowance for impairment in accordance with the PPSAS or PFRS; (b) properly provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with Section 127 of IPSAS No. 1 to provide information that may affect the fairness of the financial statements; and (c) comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 for possible filing of a request for authority to writeoff dormant accounts receivable accounts by the Local Chief Executive to the Commission on Audit.

53. Management submit the special project plan showing the outputs or deliverables per member of the MCT to determine the estimated man-hours spent by the latter, which shall serve as the basis in the computation of their honorarium and the other aforementioned lacking documents requiredunderCOA Circular No. 2012-1.
54. Henceforth, ensure that the disbursement vouchers and payrolls are supported with complete documentary requirements to establish the validity of the transactions.
observations.reason: The required documents had not yet been prepared and submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: 55. The Municipality optimally utilize the 70% LDRRMF for expenditures that would strengthen its capacity to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects on disasters or calamities as provided under RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

56. The Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned individuals and determine its collectability.
57. The MSWD Officer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in locating available documents to support the claims. Otherwise, the Municipal Accountant shall request a write-off of these accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

58. Management strictly observe and implement the regulations on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
59. Management demand from concerned employees the immediate settlement of their cash advances.

The local chief executive, together with the Sanggunian, require the refund of the excess amount granted and discontinue the granting of PEI more than the maximum allowable rate of ₱5,000.00 per personnel, as provided for in Section 1.a of E.O. No. 80 dated July 20, 2012, to avoid overpayment.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers were no longer in service and hoped that the PEI received in excess of 5,000 would not be disallowed by the auditor.

observations.recommendation:
62. The Municipal Agriculturist supervise closely the Dispersal Program and see to it that proper records are kept and that all movement (birth of offspring, death, transfers) are completely recorded in their records and reported to the Municipal Treasurer.
63. The Municipal Agriculturist assigned to manage the program keep a record of each animal acquired under the dispersal program, keep systematic records, and reports the birth of animals, deaths, and disposals to the GSO monthly using General Form No. 41 (A) – Property Inventory.
64. In case of death or disposal, the Municipal Agriculturist is required to facilitate the request for relief of accountability or to require payment of the value of the animal if warranted under the circumstances, so that accountability and liability can be imposed.
65. The Municipal Treasurer require the Municipal Agriculturist to submit a monthly report of births of animals and to post these to the property records, after which the same shall be forwarded to the accounting office for posting to the books of accounts.
66. The office of the Municipal Agriculturist to update their records and segregate active and inactive contracts.
67. The Municipal Agriculture Office and GSO reconcile their records taking into consideration the latest physical inventory conducted.
observations.reason: The Municipal Agriculturist did not submit the monthly report of births of animals, and was still updating the records of livestock dispersal.

observations.recommendation:
68. The Accounting Office and GSO reconcile their records of unserviceable properties.
69. The Municipal Treasurer prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) with the appraisal of each item conducted by an appraisal committee created for the purpose.
70. Management undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties following the procedures and guidelines provided under Section 79 of Presidential Decree1445andSections501-510 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I.
71. The Municipal Accountant drop the amount of the unserviceable properties from their books of accounts after disposal, and records the proceeds of the auction sale, if any.
observations.reason: The Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties was not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation: 72. Require the officials and other personnel concerned to refund in full the amount received by them as they are not allowed in the audit for lack of legal basis.
observations.reason: The personnel concerned were no longer in the service.

observations.recommendation:
73. Direct the Treasury, Accounting, and Legal Offices to secure the Land Titles to all real properties as an indispensable requirement. Lands owned or acquired by LGUs without Torrens Title shall, within the next fiscal year from their acquisition, be asked to be surveyed with a view to securing title thereto, and the appropriation for such survey and other requirements for securing title shall be deemed a preferred charge against the unappropriated funds during the said ensuing fiscal year (Sec. 955 of COA Circular No. 92-386).
74. Instruct the Legal Office to facilitate the application for the issuance of the TCT in favor of the Municipality of Pamplona so that the government may be shielded from lawsuits or unlawful claims over such property. Subsequently, all purchases of real property should be done in accordance with the pertinent laws governing such transactions to prevent disallowances in the audit.
75. Direct the Accountant and the Assessor to reconcile both their records so that they should come into an agreement for the Land account to be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The necessary documents for securing land titles were not available.

observations.recommendation: 76. Require the Municipal officials concerned to ensure that all real properties owned by the Municipal government are properly recorded in the books and covered by valid documents showing legal ownership thereof.
observations.reason: The real properties declared in the name of the LGU had not yet been recorded in the books of accounts because the management had not yet secured the deeds of donation and other documents to substantiate their ownership.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:
To create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical inventory of PPE and conduct the one-time cleansing;
To conduct a complete inventory count of all PPEs of the LGU and submit a Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; and
To reconcile the inventory count per RPCPPE with property and accounting records pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and undertake all other procedures prescribed under the same Circular to come up with the correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following to the Management:
In order to present the financial statements fairly, the Municipal Accountant effect the following journal entry:
The Municipal Accountant regularly reconcile the accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 112 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Paragraph 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards 1.

We recommended that the Management undertake the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of receivables to ensure their accuracy and existence;
For dormant accounts where records and documents are no longer available, conduct investigation as to the causes of the missing documents or to identify the officials and employees responsible; and
Initiate the request for write-off of the dormant accounts and comply with guidelines and procedures on the proper disposition and write-off/derecognition of dormant accounts outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended that the Management:
Set up and maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the operations of the public utility with complete subsidiary records. The Budget and Accounting Offices of the LGU shall also maintain separate registries for appropriations and obligations, respectively; and
Prepare year-end reports of the public utility, such as the Statement of Financial Position, Statement of Financial Performance and Statement of Cash Flows.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a. Update and maintain complete and accurate PPE Ledger Cards for all PPE. These records should include the acquisition date, cost, estimated useful life, and any subsequent changes affecting depreciation to properly monitor and account for asset transactions;

b. Conduct a comprehensive review of all PPE items to identify assets that have not been depreciated and ensure that depreciation is systematically applied in accordance with IPSAS 45, specifically Paragraphs 26 and 41. This includes recognizing depreciation for each significant component of an asset, where applicable; and

c. Compute the appropriate depreciation expense for the previously omitted PPE items and recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation to correct the misstatement. The necessary adjusting journal entries should be prepared and recorded to reflect the accurate net book value of the affected assets and to eliminate the overstatement in both the asset and equity accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that all cash receipts from fund transfers by the National Government are properly recorded under operating activities in the Statement of Cash Flows and should not be offset against payments to suppliers in accordance with Paragraphs 18, 19, and 22 of IPSAS 2.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a. Recognize all subsidies from the National Government as revenue in the books of accounts upon satisfaction of any conditions attached to the funds, in accordance with Paragraphs 12, 48, and 49 of IPSAS 23;

b. Record the corresponding expenses when the funds are utilized, as required by Paragraph 13, to reflect the true cost of delivering services funded by these subsidies; and

c. Avoid offsetting liabilities (Due to NGAs) directly against disbursements without recognizing the related revenue and expenses.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and provide the necessary records of unexpended balances of LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE of MF for CYs 2020 to 2024, to facilitate the transfer of cash totaling ₱17,559,915.03 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund (STF) account, in accordance with Item 5.1.10 of the said Circular.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and record the appropriate journal entries to reflect the actual transfer of funds and to correct the existing negative balance in the Cash in Bank – LCCA account under the Trust Fund books.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile and update the Schedule of CIP by identifying completed projects that remain recorded under CIP, particularly those that have been certified as completed by the Municipal Engineer but are not yet transferred to the appropriate PPE accounts.

We further recommended and the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant agreed to assign personnel to verify the items included in the CIP account that already have Certificates of Completion and to make the necessary adjustments for reclassifying these projects into their appropriate PPE accounts while recognizing the corresponding depreciation and accumulated depreciation for each completed project.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to provide the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the RPT records for CY 2024 and previous years to establish the beginning balance for CY 2025.

We further recommended and it was agreed that, henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year with a certified list of taxpayers with their amounts due and collectible for the current year, to serve as her basis for recording the RPT and SET Receivables in compliance with Sections 19(b), 20, and 84 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended and the Municipal Agriculturist agreed to provide the Municipal Accountant with an inventory of biological assets, along with the prevailing market price for each item as of the reporting date and details regarding the prevailing cost to sell these biological assets.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare JEVs for valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the GL, in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to follow the check disbursement process prescribed in Section 44 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to ensure that the JEVs are prepared and recorded based on the RCI together with the DVs and supporting documents received from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Review and analyze all dormant accounts to determine their validity and secure/gather all available documents related to these accounts;

b. If a complete review and analysis cannot be performed due to missing documents and records, she shall then prepare a list of available records pertaining to the dormant account, indicate the extent of validation conducted, and attach all documents to the list. A detailed list of all lost documents or records must be included; and

c. Prepare and submit a detailed report to the Head of Agency recommending an investigation to determine the cause/s of the missing documents and identify the official/s and employee/s responsible for the loss.

We further recommended and it was agreed that if the investigation results warrant request for authority to write-off from the Commission, the Head of the Accounting Unit, through the HoA, should file with or through the ATL and/or SA, depending on the jurisdictional amount, a request for the approval of COA to write-off or derecognize the dormant accounts from the books. The request shall be supported by the documents listed in Annex 8 of the Circular.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the omission of the accrued interest not recorded at year-end, amounting to ₱119,443.29, in accordance with Section 4.a of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. 1.

We recommended and the Department Heads of the Municipality agreed to improve their processes for monitoring financial transactions, ensuring that documents are submitted promptly to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor agreed to conduct tax mapping to cleanse their records of real properties and post the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of RPT pursuant to Section 254 of R.A 7160.

We further recommended and it was agreed that, after the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of RPT is posted, Management avail itself of the remedies provided under R.A. No. 7160 to collect delinquent taxes

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to provide the Municipal Treasurer with the records of unutilized funds under the BGCM and financial assistance for Typhoon Odette victims, so that the latter can return these funds to the National Treasury in accordance with DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020, and DBM LBC No. 141 dated December 28, 2021.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Require the responsible official to submit the airline boarding passes for travel expense claims under Disbursement Voucher (DV) Nos. 100-24-10-1716 and 100-24-12-2035. If boarding passes are no longer available, secure official certification from the respective airline(s) confirming that the concerned government official boarded their flights on the travel dates indicated in the itineraries;

b. Ensure that claims for Daily Travel Expenses are in accordance with the allowable rates set forth in EO No. 77; and

c. Provide a written explanation detailing the rationale for including individuals not employed by the Municipality in the travel expense claims amounting to ₱347,761.89, along with the legal basis for utilizing public funds to cover travel-related expenses incurred by non-municipal employees.

We recommended and Management agreed to revert the balance of the LGSEF from the Due to NGAs to the General Fund and utilize the amount in accordance with budgetary requirements and general limitations under Sections 324 and 325, respectively, of R.A. No. 7160, and other existing laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended and the Local School Board (LSB) Chairperson and Co-Chairperson agreed to:

a. Ensure that the approved SEF Budget be stamped as “received” by the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer within the indicative timeline or within seven (7) days after approval of the SEF Budget for verification of compliance with Sec. 6.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular, s. 2017;

b. Ensure the timely submission of the quarterly and annual SEF Utilization Reports to the DepEd Central Office the local sanggunian, and the DBM and DILG Regional Offices, in accordance with the Joint Circular; and

c. Ensure compliance with the posting requirements under FDP and Item 6.1 of the Joint Circular by posting the SEF Utilization Report on the LGU website and on the three Full Disclosure Policy Bulletin Boards of the Municipality not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter.

We have recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive:

Require the Inventory Committee to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality; and

Require the Inventory Committee to submit the Inventory Report using the prescribed form of Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment showing relevant information, such as property number, unit value, balance of PPE per property cards and per count pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and undertake all other procedures prescribed under the same Circular to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: The Management conducted partial inventory of its PPE accounts. | The Management did not create an Inventory Committee, hence, the non-completion of the inventory count and one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer at year-end the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRMF” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The Management, through the Municipal Accountant, transferred the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2024 and prior years’ to the Trust Liability-DRRM account.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.

We have also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer regularly monitor the implementation of the 20% Development Plan, including continuing appropriations, and closely coordinate with the concerned departments in the implementation of these projects, in order for the Municipality to fully achieve the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Some of the projects under 20% Development Fund were already implemented by the Management in CY 2024. | Management explained that the delay in the implementation of projects is due to the prioritization of prior years’ unimplemented projects.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management timely implement the funded programs or projects by the National Government in order to maximize the use of government resources and prompt delivery of the desired socio- economic outcomes to its constituents. 

We have also recommended that the Management immediately prepare the corresponding liquidation documents to be submitted to the source agency, and return the unutilized balances of the fund in accordance with Item 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Management liquidated some of the fund transfers and returned the unutilized balances to the source agencies.
observations.reason: The remaining fund transfers are yet to be implemented in CY 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: 

Record her deposits based on the RCDs and validated bank deposit slips or bank statement.  

Update the cashbook daily and reconcile the cashbook with the cash on hand at the close of each day.  

Reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records every end of the month to take up immediately the necessary corrections/ adjustments on either of the books or accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer regularly updates her cashbook and reconcile it with the records of the Municipal Accountant.
Reiterated in Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: The Management partially complied with the recommendation to support the payments for financial assistance/ donations with complete documentation. | Some of the payments for financial assistance/ donations are lacking with basic supporting documents due to non-uniform application of guidelines in the grant of burial and medical assistance and absence of a policy and guidelines on the grant of donations and other financial assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Management submit the following necessary documents to satisfy the requirements for the grant of CNA as follows:

Resolution signed by both parties incorporating the guidelines/ criteria for granting CNA Incentive;

Certificate issued by the Head of the Agency on the total amount of unencumbered savings generated from cost-cutting measures identified in the CNA, which resulted from the joint efforts of labor and management and systems/ productivity/ income improvement; and

Proof that the planned programs/ activities/ projects have been implemented and completed in accordance with targets for the year.

The Management ensure compliance with the guidelines and documentary requirements before the release/payment of CNA Incentive.

The Management fast track the implementation of all planned programs, activities or projects, which is a prerequisite in the grant of CNA Incentive.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
1.6	We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Inventory Committee to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the Municipality; and
Require the Inventory Committee to submit the Inventory Report using the prescribed form of Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment showing relevant information, such as property number, unit value, balance of PPE per property cards and per count pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and undertake all other procedures prescribed under the same Circular to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

2.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer at year-end the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRMF” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121.

3.7	We recommended that the Management:
Advise the Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers.
Initiate the request for a write-off on other receivables that remained non-moving for almost ten (10) years or more in the books, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

4.7	We recommended that the Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.
4.8	We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer regularly monitor the implementation of the 20% Development Plan, including continuing appropriations, and closely coordinate with the concerned departments in the implementation of these projects, in order for the Municipality to fully achieve the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes.

5.6	We recommended that the Management timely implement the funded programs or projects by the National Government in order to maximize the use of government resources and prompt delivery of the desired socio- economic outcomes to its constituents.
5.7	We also recommended that the Management immediately prepare the corresponding liquidation documents to be submitted to the source agency, and return the unutilized balances of the fund in accordance with Item 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.

6.10 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Record her deposits based on the RCDs and validated bank deposit slips or bank statement.
Update the cashbook daily and reconcile the cashbook with the cash on hand at the close of each day.
Reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records every end of the month to take up immediately the necessary corrections/adjustments on either of the books or accounting records.

8.8	We recommended the following:
The Management submit the following necessary documents to satisfy the requirements for the grant of CNA as follows:
Resolution signed by both parties incorporating the guidelines/criteria for granting CNA Incentive;
Certificate issued by the Head of the Agency on the total amount of unencumbered savings generated from cost-cutting measures identified in the CNA, which resulted from the joint efforts of labor and management and systems/productivity/income improvement; and
Proof that the planned programs/activities/projects have been implemented and completed in accordance with targets for the year.
The Management ensure compliance with the guidelines and documentary requirements before the release/payment of CNA Incentive.
The Management fast track the implementation of all planned programs, activities or projects, which is a prerequisite in the grant of CNA Incentive.

1.10 We recommended and Management agreed to enjoin the requesting offices
to submit their DVs, OBRs, and supporting documents on time to facilitate
the Municipal Accountant’s preparation and submission of the required
reports to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.

1.11 We further recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to take
steps to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the SAAOB. This
could entail hiring temporary staff during busy periods or re-evaluating the
workflow and deadlines to accommodate unforeseen delays.

2.8 We recommended and Management agreed to observe the guidelines and
procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020,
for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to have reliable PPE
balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability as
follows:

a. Reorganize the Inventory Committee to include members who can be
relieved of their regular duties to devote full time to conducting the
physical inventory taking until the same is completed.

b. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical
count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at the station, and
disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items and require the
Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile their records based on the
results of the actual physical inventory and effect necessary adjustments;

c. Prepare and submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team within
the prescribed period; and

d. Undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs in the
RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the
Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs
(PPE Ledger Cards) maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled.

3.17 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:

a. Create an Inventory Committee to focus on the physical count of local
roads; and

b. Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal
Engineer to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines
on the local roads asset management system pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.

4.15 We recommended that the:

a. LCE reorganize the Inventory Committee to include members who can be
relieved of their regular duties to devote full time to conducting the
physical inventory taking until the same is completed;

b. Inventory Committee prepare and submit a copy of the inventory reports to
the Auditor to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs;

c. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain the supplies
ledger cards and stock cards, respectively; and

d. Municipal Accountant strictly follow the Perpetual Inventory Method for
recording purchases of supplies and materials as required in the NGAS
Manual.

5.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revise the Notes
to the FS to include the principal amount, interest rate, maturity date,
purpose of the loan, and interest expense of the Loans Payable - Domestic
account.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed to provide additional manpower
in the Municipal Engineering Office and the necessary equipment to hasten
the implementation of the PPAs under the 20% DF.

7.9 We recommended and Management agreed to reallocate the funds from
completed and unimplemented projects that the Municipality can no longer
execute to finance disaster risk reduction and management activities
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-02, dated September 12, 2012.

7.10 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
reclassify and transfer the identified MOOE account amounting to
₱200,000.00 to the Trust Liabilities - Special Trust Fund account.

8.9 We recommended and Management agreed to utilize the procured CCTVs
exclusively for disaster risk reduction and management. Otherwise, the
LDRRMF should be reimbursed from the general fund for the cost of the
CCTV.

8.10 We further recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to the
provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17,
2012, and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1
dated March 25, 2013, on the utilization of the LDRRMF.

9.6 We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed that the LDRRM pass a resolution
reverting the CYs 2011 and 2013 LDRRMF unspent balances to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for adoption by the
Sangguniang Bayan through the Local Development Council.

9.7 We further recommended and Management agreed that the members of the
Local Finance Committee prepare the transfer of funds and accounting
reports to facilitate the reversion and certify the availability of said funds for
subsequent reappropriation for social services. Henceforth, they should
annually review the Special Trust Fund to determine the available amounts
for reversion.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


       a. implement the procedures prescribed under  COA  Circular  2020-006
          dated January 31, 2020 in the conduct of physical  count  of  PPE,
          recognition of PPE items found at station and disposition for non-
          existing/missing PPE items  for  the  one-time  cleansing  of  PPE
          account balances of government  agencies  to  produce  a  reliable
          report and PPE balances;


       b. direct the Municipal Accountant to record all  road  networks  and
          its components, based on the Registry of  Public  Infrastructures,
          or in its absence, based on  the  complete  Local  Road  Inventory
          Report provided with complete description and cost segregation  of
          the road components and provide  proper  disclosure  of  the  road
          networks in the Notes to Financial Statements;


       c. direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated  Local  Road
          Inventory and Road Map and keep  a  complete  Local  Road  Network
          Property Card of all roads within its area of jurisdiction;


       d. direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal  Accountant
          and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost
          segregation  of  all  road   components   within   its   area   of
          jurisdiction;


       e. require the Inventory Committee to conduct complete  inventory  of
          local roads, highways and bridges within its area of  jurisdiction
          which were acquired thru purchase, construction or donations;


       f. require the  Municipal  Accountant  to  provide  for  depreciation
          allowances for Infrastructure Assets  and  prepare  the  necessary
          adjusting entries in the government equity  for  the  unrecognized
          depreciation expense;


       g. strictly observe the use of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) by  the
          Property Officer in the  issuance  of  semi-expendable  items  for
          proper monitoring, control and accountability;


       h. require the Municipal Accountant to identify items  needed  to  be
          excluded from the PPE account  and  make  proper  adjustments,  if
          deemed necessary;


       i. require coordination  and  reconciliation  of  records  among  the
          Offices of  the  Municipal  Accountant,  Municipal  Treasurer  and
          Municipal Assessor for the accurate recording of  real  properties
          owned by the LGU; and


       j.  require  the  Municipal  Assessor’s  Office  to  facilitate   the
          registration of the untitled land and/or transfer of title of  the
          aforementioned parcels of lots in the name of  the  LGU  with  the
          Registry of Deeds.

In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended  the  Management
to:

     a. require the adoption of the  perpetual  inventory  method  and  the
        maintenance of Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards as provided in
        the NGAS Manual for LGUs;


     b.  ensure  the  implementation  of  moving-average  costing  in   the
        valuation of inventory accounts; and


     c. enjoin the conduct of semi-annual physical inventory  counts  based
        on the provisions on the Manual and the timely submission of RPCI.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, Property Officer, and
Municipal Engineer agreed to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting
guidelines on local road asset management systems and earnestly perform their
respective duties and responsibilities set forth under Item VII of COA Circular
No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.

We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to create an
Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the properties of the
Municipality and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN and RPCPPE.
Likewise, we recommended that the results of the physical count be reconciled
with the related property and accounting records in accordance with the
pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to
ensure the accuracy of the reported PPE balances in the financial statements.
Any discrepancies noted in the reconciliation shall be adjusted in accordance
with Section 7.0 and 8.0 of the Circular as a one-time cleansing of the PPE
account balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the
subsidiary ledgers of each accountable officer as of December 31, 2021, monitor
cash advances, and demand the immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash
advances by issuing demand letters direct to the accountable officers concerned.
Otherwise, if no liquidation is made, impose the sanction of withholding the
salaries of those who still fail to settle their accounts after due notice.

We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require all
concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their cash advances and
direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation of cash
advances within the prescribed period and see to it that no additional cash
advances are granted unless the previous ones are settled or properly accounted
for.

Henceforth, we recommended and Management agreed to comply with
the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash
advances in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-
002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop from making further
disbursements for the improvement of the water system unless the ownership of
the water system as well as the land on which it is situated is transferred to the
Municipality.

We further recommended and Management agreed to desist from disbursing
government funds for private purposes pursuant to Section 335 of R.A. No. 7160 and
COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly
observe compliance with the perpetual inventory method of accounting for
construction supplies and materials procured for projects implemented by
administration by recording all purchases under the “Construction Materials
Inventory.”

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
coordinate with the property custodian to implement controls on the receipt and
utilization of the same, to submit a summary of the utilized construction
materials to the Accounting Office, and effect the necessary adjustment for the
cost of supplies and materials which remained unutilized for the intended
projects.

We finally recommended and Management agreed to take up a receivable
account from the water associations for the amount incurred by the
Municipality for the improvement of the water systems.

We recommended and Management agreed that in the planning phase,
the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Development
Council (MDC), review the feasibility of the implementation of the identified
PPAs to be funded by the 20% DF with corresponding quantifiable targets
which can be implemented within the desired time frame to maximize the
utilization of the 20% IRA. Further, we recommended and Management agreed
to properly identify the priority projects under the 20% DF in accordance with
Section 3.2.3 of the DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated
November 4, 2020.

We further recommended and Management agreed that the status of
implementation of the projects be included in the agenda of the MDC’s regular
meeting to promote greater transparency and accountability in the LGU’s
appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF pursuant to Section 3.2.2 of JMC
No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to discontinue charging the
monthly remuneration of community-based farm-to-market road workers to the
70% LDRRMF, as these are considered maintenance and operating activities of
the Municipality. Maintenance and other operating expenses shall be
appropriately charged against the regular budget or funds of the Municipality.
Henceforth, utilize the 70% LDRRMF for more worthy projects and programs
that would strengthen its capacity to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of
disaster or calamity.

We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to coordinate with the OCD
on the preparation and submission of the Municipality’s PSCP pursuant to
NDRRMC Memorandum No. 33, s. 2018.

We further recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the
following activities:
a. To conduct risk assessment;
b. To identify critical processes and functions;
c. To determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operation;
d. To conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
e. To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical
processes and functions and its related steps to be followed to
eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the determined
disruptions following the prescribed templates and
requirements in the PSCP guidebook; and
f. To submit plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the Local Chief
Executive for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the
passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the contractor to
prepare the As-Built Plan for review and approval by the Municipal Engineer
and the Municipal Mayor or his duly authorized representative, respectively, for
this project and henceforth, before final payment and acceptance.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements before payment of the
final billing as provided under Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012.

We furthermore recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013, on the
specifications for the signboards, billboards, and tarpaulins to be posted at
venues where the projects/programs and activities of the local government unit
shall be implemented to fully adopt the full disclosure policy of all transactions
governing the public interest.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the
suppliers/dealers to submit the Tax and Duty Receipt corresponding to the
equipment procured in accordance with Section 2 of A.O. No. 200 dated
November 21, 1990.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to see to
it that, henceforth, one percent (1%) warranty security is deducted from the
amount paid to suppliers to protect the interest of the LGU pursuant to Section
62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the limitations
set under Section 45 of the GAA for cultural and athletic activities and exercise
fiscal prudence in the allocation and utilization of government funds for
programs and projects that benefit the constituents’ socioeconomic well-being,
including but not limited to economic livelihood projects, basic health services,
and socially significant projects aimed at improving the common man’s socio-
economic well-being. We further recommended and Management agreed to
follow the austerity measures provided under Administrative Order No. 103.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the
specific guidelines on the grants and releases of government funds to NGOs/POs
as provided under Section 4.0 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25,
2007. We recommended further and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure
that the transfer of funds to an NGO/PO is completely supported by the
documentary requirements provided under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to ascertain the propriety and validity of the
grant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) conduct a thorough verification and analysis of the dormant receivable accounts and provide for an allowance for impairment in accordance with PPSAS/PFRS; (b) properly provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with Section 127 of IPSAS No. 1 to provide information that may affect the fairness of the financial statements; and (c) comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 for possible filing of a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts by the Local Chief Executive to the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: An allowance for impairment of Other Receivables had been provided.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not yet filed a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts; gathering of supporting documents is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require the Revenue Collection Clerk to submit the RAAF with Money Value, Invoice and Receipt of Accountable Forms from the date when the Revenue Collection Officer handled the cash tickets to the Municipal Treasurer and maintain a permanent logbook/record. Also, implement control measures such as stamping serial numbers and affixing initials/date on cash tickets before issuance.
observations.managementAction: A permanent logbook/record has been maintained by the liquidating officer. Partial payments on unaccounted cash tickets were remitted to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The required RAAF with Money Value and Invoice Receipt of Accountable Forms from the date the former Revenue Collection Officer handled the cash tickets were not submitted; thus, full accountability could not be established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management review the availability of technical personnel, equipment, and feasibility of implementing the projects within realistic timelines in identifying the PPAs to be funded by the 20% DF and hasten the implementation of eligible PPAs to attain socio-economic development as intended in the establishment of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Management allocated funds and initiated procurement; some projects were undertaken while others were planned.
observations.reason: Based on the Report of Government Projects/Programs and Activities submitted by the Municipal Engineer, the 20% DF was still not optimally utilized; several projects remained unimplemented or ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the disbursement vouchers and payrolls for honoraria are supported with complete documentary requirements. The municipal officials should prepare a special project plan showing outputs or deliverables per project component and estimated man-hours to serve as basis in computing honoraria.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared special project plans and computed honoraria per deliverable.
observations.reason: Documentation was improved but some required supporting documents and computation details were still lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize the 70% LDRRMF for expenditures that would strengthen its capacity to avoid or mitigate adverse effects of disasters or calamities. Maintenance and other operating expenses shall be charged to the regular budget under the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management purchased the ambulance and paid remunerations; funds were reallocated for LDRRMF projects.
observations.reason: Some expenditures for administrative purposes continued to be charged to the LDRRMF; adjustments were ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the SWMB review and update the MSWMP as required under Section 16 of RA No. 9003 and submit the same to the NSWMC for review and approval. Prepare Closure and Rehabilitation Plans for existing open dumpsites and ensure compliance with DENR rules.
observations.managementAction: The SWMB was tasked to review and update the MSWMP and prepare required plans.
observations.reason: Updating of the SWMP was still on-going. The agency action plan on the status of implementation of the audit recommendation was not submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee prioritize its conduct of physical inventory of properties and accomplish the necessary reports annually in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS, Volume I. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should update the AREs and maintain property cards. The Accounting Office should update the PPE ledger cards and the Schedule of PPE and periodically reconcile these records.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was instructed to conduct the physical count and reconcile records; partial counts and updates were being undertaken.
observations.reason: Inventory count on PPEs of the Municipality was not conducted completely; RPCPPE was not prepared and submitted. Reconciliation between records and physical counts remained pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWD Officer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in locating available documents to support the claims. Otherwise, the Municipal Accountant shall request authority to write-off these accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The MSWD Office coordinated with the Municipal Accountant to locate available documents and follow-up on collection; some documents were being traced.
observations.reason: Supporting documents were being located; no request for write-off had yet been filed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management observe and implement the regulations on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Demand immediate settlement of outstanding advances and strictly enforce liquidation procedures.
observations.managementAction: Management issued demand notices to concerned accountable officers and implemented stricter monitoring of cash advances.
observations.reason: Advances for Operating Expenses, Payroll, and Advances to Officers and Employees still had an outstanding balance of ₱6,773,591.36 as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties and ensure compliance with RA No. 9003 by involving all Barangays in waste reduction and segregation at source and by implementing a sanitary landfill plan.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality purchased a lot for establishment of a sanitary landfill and prepared action plans for implementation.
observations.reason: Open dumpsite disposal continued; full implementation of landfill plans and compliance with RA No. 9003 remained in progress. Agency action plan for CY 2021 was not submitted to Auditor for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with documentary requirements under Section 5.7.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 before granting honoraria and ensure disbursement vouchers and payrolls are properly supported.
observations.managementAction: Management started complying with the documentary requirements for subsequent payments and ensured proper documentation.
observations.reason: Some documentary requirements were still lacking for prior payments; improvements implemented for subsequent disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer direct the General Services Officer to mark all vehicles and transportation equipment owned by the Municipality with "For Official Use Only" pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.managementAction: Some vehicles were marked "For Official Use Only"; GSO was instructed to complete marking.
observations.reason: Some vehicles were still not marked; ongoing action to mark remaining vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasury prepare and maintain perpetual inventory records, conduct physical counts, and properly record inventory transactions following NGAS guidance.
observations.managementAction: Perpetual inventory methods were adopted and inventory records were being updated.
observations.reason: Physical inventory was conducted for some items; full reconciliation and perpetual records were still being updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan refund the excess amounts and desist from granting PEI beyond the allowable rate in the future. Require refund of excess amounts and follow DBM guidance.
observations.managementAction: Management initiated refund collections from employees who received excess PEI; partial refunds were made.
observations.reason: Refunds were being processed on an installment basis; complete recovery was ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended cessation of charging prior years’ electricity expenses against current year appropriations. If funds are available, require the Sangguniang Bayan to make a supplemental budget so that unpaid obligations can be settled properly. Exercise prudence in spending and control electricity use.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the practice of charging prior years' expenses and prepared adjustments; a supplemental budget was considered.
observations.reason: Some prior year electricity obligations were still being processed; improvements being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipal Agriculturist supervise closely the Dispersal Program and ensure that proper records are kept and that all movement (birth of offspring, death, transfers) are completely recorded in their records and reported to the Municipal Treasurer monthly using General Form No. 41 (A) – Property Work/Other Inventory. b. In case of death or disposal, the Municipal Agriculturist facilitate the request for relief from accountability or to require payment of the value of the animal if warranted. c. The Municipal Treasurer require monthly reports and posting to property records for accounting. d. The office of the Municipal Agriculturist update records and segregate active and inactive contracts and reconcile with GSO and Accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist was instructed to maintain systematic records of livestock dispersal, births, deaths, and disposals and coordinate with Municipal Treasurer for proper recording; partial compliance observed.
observations.reason: A physical inventory was partially conducted; reconciliation between Municipal Agriculturist and Accounting/GSO records was still on-going. Some documentation remained lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Accounting Office and GSO reconcile records and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) with appraisal of each item by an appraisal committee. b. The Municipal Treasurer prepare the IIRUP and initiate disposal as per PD 1445 and GAAM procedures. c. Management dispose of unserviceable properties immediately and record proceeds from auction sales and drop disposed items from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: An IIRUP was being prepared; lot of unserviceable items were identified and appraisal initiated. Disposal procedures were being followed.
observations.reason: IIRUP was not completed; unserviceable properties were not yet disposed of; auction or disposal in compliance with GAAM remained pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, pursuant to Sections 182, 187 and 189 Volume 1 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to set up the proper safeguards on various supplies, drugs and medicines that:

a. The General Services Officer maintain stock cards for inventories received, issue Requisition and Issue Slip for the issuance of inventories and prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the required Report of Materials and Supplies Issued five days after the end of the month.

b. The Municipal Accountant maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each specific type of Inventories.

c.	The Municipal Mayor require periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. The physical count of unissued inventory items by type shall be conducted at least once a year and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendations.
Management already created an Inventory Committee and has started the one-time cleansing of PPE but due to Covid-19 restrictions,
observations.reason: cleansing was not finished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer and record all completed projects funded from the Trust Fund in the General Fund books and recognize the corresponding depreciation expenses thereon in accordance with the recognition and measurement guidelines set out by IPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management the following courses of action:

a. For the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:

1. Require the Barangay Governments to submit their

proposal with complete details before the completion of the Annual Investment Plan.

2. Initiate the preparation of necessary documents to support cost estimate of projects funded under the 20% Development Plan before the budget preparation.

3.Develop and recommend other procedures to enhance the current practice regarding the planning and budgeting of the 20% Development Plan to avoid generic and lump sum projects.

b.	 For the Office Local Chief Executive to:

1. 	Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lump sum projects.
2. 	Stop the practice of implementing generic or lump sum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management reprogrammed the appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents for the months of October to December 2020 and payroll for the month of December 2020, henceforth strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 on the submission of accounts and other reports of the municipality on or before the set deadline, to avoid administrative sanctions and/or disallowance of said accounts, as provided for in Section 39 (1) & (3) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the bonding requirement pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 and pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 to protect government funds against losses through force majeure.
observations.managementAction: Accountable Officers already applied for the renewal of their bonds but were not yet confirmed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a.	GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the municipality’s road network system;

b.	Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects; and thereafter,

c.	Municipal Accountant to record amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.
observations.managementAction: The management recorded in partial its Local Road Networks in the books of accounts.
observations.reason:
Management
could no fully implement the audit recommendation due to the absence of some documents pertaining to Local Road Networks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the concerned department heads agreed to strictly
comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on local roads asset
management system, and to earnestly perform their respective duties and
responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23,
2015.

We also recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct annual physical count
of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be
submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each year.
The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records and the necessary
adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to provide
disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or the Notes to FS the changes
between the original and final budgets and reconciliation of actual amounts per
SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the
Municipality’s financial statements. Likewise, adequate disclosure shall be
provided in the Notes to FS for the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to
paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Budget Officer reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the
accumulation of reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of balances
between the SCBAA and FS.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from transferring
monthly IRA from LBP to DBP unless approved by BTr in accordance with the
pertinent provisions of BTR Circular No. 01-2017 dated December 28, 2016. If
for any valid reason the transfer of IRA is necessary, we recommended that
Management submit their request for transfer of the depository bank for IRA,
together with the documentary requirements enumerated under Section 2.4 of
same Treasury Circular, to the Bureau of Treasury-Province/District Office
concerned for processing and submission to the Regional Director for final
action.

We recommended and Management agreed to negotiate for the most
advantageous price for the government based on the existing data of DOH or
other relevant agencies in accordance with Section 5 of GPPB Circular No. 2020-
001 dated April 6, 2020. We also recommended and Management agreed to
require concerned personnel or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the
Procuring Entity to completely and properly fill up the relevant and required
information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated
August 15, 1996.

We recommended that the LDRRMO agreed to submit to the Audit
Team the original copies of the RDS. Likewise, we recommended that in the
distribution of meals and other goods, Management indicate all the necessary
information in the distribution sheet, particularly the specific goods and
quantity of items distributed as well as the date of receipt, before causing the
beneficiaries to sign in the acknowledgement section thereof, to properly
establish accountability and account for the goods procured.

We recommended and the BAC, through its Secretariat, agreed to post in
the GPPB online portal the updated APP, NOA and other information relative
to the procurement of projects in accordance with the provisions of GPPB
Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020. We also recommended that the LCE
send a written notice to the Auditor and other government agencies stated under
Section 4.4 of DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 on the posting of NOA,
Contract or Purchase Orders and other necessary information in the GPPB
Online Portal.

We recommended and Management agreed that repairs and maintenance
of motor vehicles and communication expenses of the LDRRMO shall be
sourced from the General Fund of the LGU. We also recommended that food
and transportation expenses (including fuel) of medical and other LGU
personnel directly involved in the implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs,
and other related expenses shall be charged to BGCM and 20% DF throughout
the duration of the State of Calamity as declared by the President through
Proclamation No. 929. We further recommended that the unexpended balances
of the LDRRMF shall be utilized for PPAs stipulated in the NDRRMC, DBM
and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 to
strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to avoid or mitigate the adverse
effects of disasters or calamity.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare and furnish
the Auditor a copy of the (a) acknowledgement receipts of the donations in-
kind; (b) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (c)
inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with
Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization and include in the report all
information required in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require
the MDRRMO to submit the said report on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor
photocopy of the identification card with specimen signature of the beneficiaries
and photos of the beneficiaries during actual receipt of the assistance pursuant
to Item 9 of the MOA between the DSWD and the Municipality dated April 24,
2020 as well as any proof of authenticity provided under Section VII.A.1 of
DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020 dated April 9, 2020. We
further recommended that that Municipal Treasurer submit the authority from
the beneficiaries for claims received other than the payees.

We recommended and Management agreed to refund the ESP assistance
received by the JOs and other contracted personnel amounting to ₱30,000.00 as
they are ineligible to receive the same pursuant to Section VI-B of DSWD
Memorandum of Circular No. 09 series of 2020 dated April 9, 2020. We also
recommended compliance with applicable guidelines on the implementation of
the ESP of the DSWD.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor copy
of the (a) Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), (b) Report on the Sources of
Fund and Utilization, (c) photocopy of the identification card with specimen
signature of the beneficiaries, (d) photos of the beneficiaries during actual
receipt of the assistance and (e) copy of Official Receipt for any refund of
unutilized balance. We further recommended that that Municipal Treasurer
submit the authority from the beneficiaries and other documentary
requirements provided under Item VIII(D.4) of DSWD Memorandum Circular
No. 04 series of 2019 dated January 31, 2019 for claims received other than the
social pensioners.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to require the suppliers to submit
the original notarized OSS before or after award of contract but before payment
is made and include a warranty clause in the contract or POs for procurements
through emergency purchase in accordance withh GPPB Circular No. 01-2020
dated April 6, 2020. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for
emergency procurement before payment is made.

The General Services Officer maintain stock cards for inventories received, issue Requisition and Issue Slip for the issuance of inventories and prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the required Report of Materials and Supplies Issued five days after the end of the month.
